premise.rawLogline: When a glassblower's apprentice witnesses a murder reflected in a window she's just made, she becomes the only person who can identify the killer — and the only person they're hunting.
premise.conceptHook: The witness who can never un-see, hunted by what she saw.

protagonist.name: Mira Halloran
protagonist.dailyLife: Six days a week at the studio, alone most evenings, sister calls Sundays.
protagonist.want: To be taken seriously by her master and finish her apprenticeship
protagonist.need: To stop performing competence and admit she's terrified of being seen
protagonist.ghost: Her sister survived an attack ten years ago and Mira didn't tell the police what she saw
protagonist.flaw: Looks away from things that matter
protagonist.coreLie: If I don't see it, it isn't real
protagonist.arcDirection: positive — from witness who hides to witness who speaks

characters.name: Det. Arthur Vance
characters.want: To close the case before retirement
characters.need: To trust someone with what he can't prove
characters.ghost: His partner died on the job; he never gave the eulogy
characters.relationshipToProtagonist: investigating detective who slowly trusts her
characters.arcSummary: From paternal scepticism to genuine partnership
characters.meetsProtagonistAt: Catalyst — interviews her after the murder

characters.name: Coen Halloran
characters.relationshipToProtagonist: younger sister; the one who survived
characters.arcSummary: Catalyst for protagonist's reckoning with the past

characters.name: Anton Reed
characters.ghost: First victim was someone he loved; now he disposes of anyone who could have saved her
characters.arcSummary: Escalation — from observer to direct threat

relationships.conflict: He needs her to remember; she needs not to
relationships.whatTheyWantFromEachOther: Mira wants protection without explanation; Vance wants identification

relationships.conflict: Coen wants Mira to face what happened ten years ago; Mira refuses

logline.sentence: When a glassblower's apprentice realises the murder she just witnessed in reflection mirrors the attack on her sister a decade earlier, she must choose between disappearing again or testifying — even though the killer has already learned her name.
logline.setup: Mira is months from finishing her apprenticeship.
logline.incitingIncident: She sees a murder reflected in a pane she's just shaped.
logline.stakes: If she runs, the killer goes free; if she testifies, he comes for her.
logline.resolutionHint: She testifies — but only after Coen forces her to look at what she's been refusing to see.
logline.antagonistQuestion: Can the killer find her before she finds the courage to be a witness?

beat01OpeningImage.scene: Mira shaping a pane at dawn — the studio's quiet ritual.

beat02Setup.scene: A week of apprenticeship; the master's tests; the call from Coen.
beat02Setup.themeStated: What you see is what you owe

beat03Catalyst.scene: Murder reflected in a finished pane; Mira freezes.

beat04Debate.scene: Should she report it? Vance's first interview.

beat05BreakIntoTwo.scene: She decides to call Vance and identify him.
beat05BreakIntoTwo.falseReality: If I help once, I can return to the studio.

beat06BStory.scene: Coen visits; the past starts breaking through.

beat07FunAndGames.scene: Mira cooperates with Vance; small wins.

beat08Midpoint.scene: She realises the killer matches her sister's attacker.

beat09BadGuysCloseIn.scene: Anton learns her name. Studio break-in.

beat10AllIsLost.scene: Coen is attacked. Mira lied to herself.
beat10AllIsLost.darkNightOfSoul: What she didn't see ten years ago let this happen

beat11BlackMoment.scene: Mira flees; tries to disappear again.

beat12Beat13.scene: She returns to the studio and uses the pane.

beat13Finale.scene: Trap at the studio; Anton arrested.

beat14FinalImage.scene: Mira finishes the pane she started in chapter 1.

beat15EndCredits.scene: Coen stands beside her in the gallery.

beatSheet.overallNotes: Whydunit variant — readers see the killer early; the question is whether Mira will testify.

bStory.premise: The sister forces the protagonist to face the past she's been refusing to witness.

beats.resolves: Hospital scene after All Is Lost

bStory.resolution: Coen survives; the sisters speak about the original attack for the first time.
bStory.themeConnection: Witnessing is a relationship, not a posture.

highLevel.highLevelSummary: Studio life, sister's call, the master's expectations.
The murder; the interview; the decision to cooperate.

plotThreads.name: Identity of the killer
plotThreads.resolutionPlan: Mira's reflection-evidence at the studio trap

plotThreads.name: Coen's attack ten years ago
plotThreads.resolutionPlan: Hospital scene + sisters' first conversation about it

scenes.summary: Mira works alone at dawn; the master arrives.
scenes.conflict: She makes a small mistake she covers up
scenes.whatChanges: She agrees to take on a private commission

scenes.summary: Coen calls; Mira refuses to talk about the past.
scenes.whatChanges: Coen says she's coming this weekend regardless

scenes.summary: She finishes a pane and sees the murder.
scenes.conflict: Internal — does she trust what she saw
